scope.summary:
Goal: make every document, rich-text block, canvas node, and code-file node in the native surface addressable as a Loom block with a content hash and live backlinks. This is the 'everything is a Loom block' invariant from the interconnection contract. Expected diff: (a) a new module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_address.rs (~200–300 lines) that holds LoomBlockAddr, LoomBlockRecord, ContentHash, and the resolution logic; (b) a LoomBlockResolver that calls the existing backend APIs (getLoomBlock, getLoomBlockTransclusion, getDocumentBacklinks); (c) a BacklinkPanel widget added to the bottom of the rich-text editor and the graph pane; (d) tests proving that creating a rich document yields a loom:// URI and that the backlink panel loads real backlinks from the live PG backend.

Files to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_address.rs  — defines LoomBlockAddr { workspace_id: String, block_id: String }, ContentHash(String), LoomBlockRecord { addr: LoomBlockAddr, title: String, content_hash: ContentHash, block_type: LoomBlockType }, LoomBlockType enum { RichDocument | CanvasNode | CodeFileNode | GraphNode }. Provides fn loom_uri(addr: &LoomBlockAddr) -> String (format: "loom://{workspace_id}/{block_id}"). Provides fn parse_loom_uri(s: &str) -> Option<LoomBlockAddr>.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backlink_panel.rs  — egui widget; takes workspace_id + document_id; on first render calls backend GET /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}/backlinks; shows list of RichDocBacklink { backlink_id, source_document_id, link_kind, target, block_id }; each row is a clickable button that fires the OpenDocument command via the shared command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, established in MT-031). AccessKit: panel container author_id="backlinks-panel-{doc_id}", role=List; each row author_id="backlink-{backlink_id}", role=ListItem, actions=[Press].

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs  — add `pub mod loom_address;` and `pub mod backlink_panel;` module declarations (the native crate uses flat module registration in lib.rs; there is no shared/mod.rs or widgets/mod.rs).
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/editor.rs  — after saving a document, call backend PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id} with the document's content_hash (derived as SHA-256 of canonical JSON); embed BacklinkPanel at the bottom of the pane. (rich_text/ is the new WP-012 rich-text editor module subdir under handshake_native/src/.)
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs  — each canvas node that maps to a placed Loom block must resolve and display its loom:// address in the node header; double-click opens the referenced document. (canvas_board.rs is the existing WP-011 canvas surface; reuse it, do not create a new canvas pane.)
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs  — each graph node tooltip shows its loom:// URI and backlink count. (loom_graph.rs is the existing WP-011 graph surface; reuse it.)

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. ContentHash: use sha2::Sha256 (add sha2 ^0.10 to Cargo.toml if not present). Canonical JSON: serialize the document's ProseMirror JSONContent using serde_json with sorted keys (use indexmap if needed) then hash bytes.
  2. LoomBlockAddr is stable across renames: the block_id is the backend's UUID; the workspace_id is the app-level workspace UUID.
  3. To resolve a document_id to its loom block_id: the existing backend RichDocument (from loadRichDocument / GET /knowledge/documents/{id}) already carries a block_id field (see api.ts LoomBlock: block_id). Read this field on document load.
  4. BacklinkPanel loading: call GET /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}/backlinks — this maps to the React call getRichDocBacklinks (api.ts line ~3326: request('/knowledge/documents/${documentId}/backlinks')). Response is { source_document_id, backlinks: RichDocBacklink[] }. Cache the result in BacklinkPanel's local egui state (load once per document_id change).
  5. Backlink row: display source_document_id (truncated), link_kind badge, and a right-arrow icon. On click: dispatch the "open-document" command via the shared command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) with CommandArgs::DocumentId(source_document_id). The OpenDocument command must be registered on the command bus (MT-031).
  6. Canvas node addressing: each CanvasNode in the DB has a placed_block_id (see api.ts CanvasPlacedNode line ~1642). Use this as the loom block_id. On canvas_board render, for each node, show a loom:// chip in the node header if placed_block_id is Some.
  7. Content hash write-back: after saveRichDocument returns (api.ts ~3245), read the save_receipt_event_id and doc_version. Derive ContentHash of the saved JSON. Call PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id} with { content_hash }. This is the backend updateLoomBlock call (api.ts ~1890: request('/loom/blocks/${blockId}', { method: 'PATCH', body })). Map to the Rust IPC/backend client (backend_client.rs).
  8. AccessKit: see above under BacklinkPanel; reuse the accessibility/* helpers (accessibility/live.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/snapshot.rs) for node registration.
  9. Tests: src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_loom_address.rs — (a) unit: parse_loom_uri("loom://ws1/block1") returns Some(LoomBlockAddr { workspace_id: 'ws1', block_id: 'block1' }); (b) unit: loom_uri round-trips; (c) integration (real PG): create a rich document via backend API, load it, assert block_id is non-empty, assert GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/backlinks returns 200; (d) integration: create doc A with a wikilink to doc B, save A, assert B's backlinks include A's block_id.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-031 (shared command bus / OpenDocument command registration on command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) merged.
  - MT-021 (graph surface) and MT-022 (canvas surface placing loom blocks) merged so canvas node placed_block_id field is accessible.
  - sha2 crate ^0.10 added to Cargo.toml.
  - Backend: GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/backlinks endpoint must exist (it does — confirmed in api.ts line 3326).
  - Backend: PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id} must exist (confirmed api.ts updateLoomBlock line 1890).

Risk if missed: without the loom block addressing layer, MT-033 (CKC embeds as loom:// refs) and MT-034 (code<->note cross-refs) both lack a stable addressable target, and the parity proof suite (MT-044) cannot assert the 'everything is a Loom block' invariant.
acceptance_criteria: loom_uri / parse_loom_uri round-trip: parse_loom_uri(loom_uri(&addr)) == Some(addr) — proven by unit test.
Creating a rich document via the live PG backend and loading it returns a non-empty block_id that parses as a valid LoomBlockAddr — proven by integration test against real PG.

BacklinkPanel renders a list of backlinks for a document that has at least one inbound wikilink, with each row showing source_document_id and link_kind — proven by integration test creating doc A (wikilink to B) then loading B's panel.

Clicking a backlink row fires the OpenDocument command on the shared command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) with the correct document ID — proven by kittest harness asserting command receipt.
Canvas nodes with a placed_block_id display the loom:// chip in their header — proven by kittest screenshot + AccessKit node present.

Content hash write-back: after saving a rich document the backend PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id} is called with a non-empty content_hash — proven by integration test asserting updated block.content_hash matches SHA-256 of saved JSON.
AccessKit nodes 'backlinks-panel-{doc_id}' (List) and at least one 'backlink-{backlink_id}' (ListItem) present when panel has data — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
cargo test -p handshake-native loom_address -- --nocapture passes with no failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native loom_address -- --nocapture  =>  all PASS
integration test (real PG): create rich doc, load, assert block_id non-empty, GET backlinks returns 200
integration test: create doc A with wikilink [[doc_B]], save, assert GET /knowledge/documents/{B_id}/backlinks returns entry with source_document_id == A_id
kittest: click backlink row, assert shared command bus received OpenDocument command with correct doc_id
AccessKit tree dump: assert 'backlinks-panel-*' List node and at least one 'backlink-*' ListItem present
implementation_notes: sha2 = { version = "^0.10", features = [] } — add to [dependencies] in Cargo.toml.

Canonical JSON for hashing: use serde_json::to_string_pretty is NOT canonical (key order may differ). Use a sorted-keys serializer or indexmap to ensure deterministic output before hashing; otherwise two structurally identical documents may hash differently across platforms.

Cross-WP reuse (WP-KERNEL-011): the shared selection/clipboard/command bus is command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs; the OpenDocument command must be registered there (MT-031). Backend HTTP calls route through backend_client.rs. AccessKit node registration reuses accessibility/live.rs, accessibility/registry.rs, accessibility/snapshot.rs.

React reference for backlinks: LoomBlockPanel.tsx (api.ts getLoomBlock, updateLoomBlock) and LoomTransclusionView.tsx (getLoomBlockTransclusion) show how block_id is threaded through the frontend. The direct backlinks call is api.ts line 3326 getRichDocBacklinks.

React reference for placed_block_id: CanvasView.tsx line ~1642 CanvasPlacedNode type has canvas_block_id + placed_block_id. In native, mirror this from the canvas graph response (GET /canvas/{id} returns CanvasWithGraph which includes placed nodes with placed_block_id).
Do NOT cache backlinks longer than the document session; invalidate on saveRichDocument completion so the panel refreshes after the user edits links.

REUSE, DO NOT DUPLICATE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23 - CRITICAL anti-overlap): a backlinks panel ALREADY EXISTS - MT-015 built rich_editor/wikilinks/backlinks_panel.rs (GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/backlinks, with the no-perpetual-spinner fix + 404->empty already done) AND MT-024 built a sidebar Backlinks section. Do NOT create a new src/backlink_panel.rs (the contract's file) - that would be a THIRD duplicate. MT-032's UNIQUE deliverable is: (1) the loom_address.rs module (LoomBlockAddr, ContentHash, loom_uri/parse_loom_uri, LoomBlockResolver) - the 'everything is a Loom block' addressing layer; (2) loom:// chips + backlink-count on canvas nodes (graph/canvas_board.rs MT-026 placements with placed_block_id) and graph nodes (loom_graph.rs tooltips). EMBED/reuse the EXISTING MT-015 backlinks panel where the contract says 'BacklinkPanel' (extend it if needed); do not fork it. Reuse MT-024's sidebar too.

CONTENT-HASH IS LIKELY BACKEND-COMPUTED, NOT CLIENT-WRITABLE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - the MT-022 field-existence + MT-020 canonical-hash lesson): the contract says PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id} {content_hash}. But MT-022 VERIFIED LoomBlockUpdate exposes only pinned/favorite/journal_date/pin_order (NO content_hash/content_json field), and MT-020 found the backend computes the canonical-JSON hash server-side (knowledge_canonical_json_sha256). VERIFY read-only whether content_hash is a WRITABLE field on PATCH /loom/blocks/{id}; if it is NOT (likely), the backend computes content_hash on save - so READ it from the loaded LoomBlock (getLoomBlock), do NOT client-PATCH a hash (no fake/unsupported write). If a real content-hash write route exists, bind it; else the loom_address ContentHash READS the backend's hash (or recomputes matching MT-020's canonical_hash.rs algorithm for display only). REUSE MT-020's canonical_hash.rs + the existing sha2 dep (already in the tree) - do NOT reinvent canonical JSON with indexmap or add a duplicate sha2.

BACKEND PROOF + PATHS (MT-008/015/020/022 pattern): loom_uri/parse_loom_uri round-trip + ContentHash determinism are unit-testable STANDALONE; the create-doc + backlinks-load + (any) hash round-trip are REAL-PG -> bind real + gate behind integration + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. Paths: rich_text/editor.rs -> the rich-text editor is rich_editor/ (save write-back lives in MT-020's save path / rich_editor_widget; embed the backlinks via the EXISTING MT-015 panel). canvas_board.rs -> graph/canvas_board.rs (MT-026, placed_block_id). loom_graph.rs is the WP-011 graph surface (tooltips). backend_client.rs for the LoomBlockResolver (reuse the existing Loom clients). 404 backlinks -> empty 'No backlinks yet' (already handled by MT-015's panel). placed_block_id None -> skip the loom:// chip (no panic, RISK-3). NO Color32 literal (theme guard); screenshots EXTERNAL; accesskit variant (reuse MT-015's backlinks-panel author_ids, do NOT mint colliding duplicates).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomBlockPanel.tsx — getLoomBlock, updateLoomBlock, block_id threading, pinned/favorite state pattern
app/src/components/LoomTransclusionView.tsx — getLoomBlockTransclusion, resolved/unresolved/failed states for block resolution
app/src/components/CanvasView.tsx — CanvasPlacedNode type, placed_block_id field, canvas node rendering
binds_backend_api: GET /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}/backlinks  =>  api.ts line ~3326 getRichDocBacklinks  =>  returns { source_document_id, backlinks: RichDocBacklink[] }
PATCH /loom/blocks/{block_id}  =>  api.ts line ~1890 updateLoomBlock  =>  body: { content_hash }
GET /loom/blocks/{block_id}  =>  api.ts line ~1611 getLoomBlock  =>  returns LoomBlock { block_id, content_hash, ... }
GET /canvas/{canvas_id}  =>  api.ts line ~1639 getCanvas  =>  returns CanvasWithGraph including placed nodes with placed_block_id

risks: Non-deterministic JSON serialization causes content hashes to differ for the same document across runs, making the write-back useless or noisy.
Backend GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/backlinks returns 404 for new documents that have never had links; the panel must handle this gracefully without error banner.
placed_block_id may be null for canvas nodes that were created before loom-address support; display of loom:// chip must be conditional, not panic on None.
BacklinkPanel loading blocks the egui frame if the HTTP call is synchronous; must be async with a loading state.
Content hash write-back races with the saveRichDocument call if the user saves rapidly; a second save must wait for the PATCH to complete or be debounced.
minimum_controls: Use a sorted-keys JSON serializer (e.g. serde_json with BTreeMap round-trip or indexmap) and assert determinism in unit tests by hashing the same struct twice and comparing.
Handle 404 from GET backlinks as an empty list (not an error); show 'No backlinks yet' placeholder text.
Guard placed_block_id access with .as_deref().unwrap_or_default() and skip chip rendering if empty string.
Load BacklinkPanel data in a Tokio spawn or equivalent non-blocking task; store result in Arc<Mutex<BacklinkState>> polled in update().
Debounce content hash write-back: only fire PATCH if the new hash differs from the last known hash stored in pane state.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-015 backlinks panel + MT-024 sidebar + MT-020 canonical_hash + MT-022 LoomBlockUpdate limits + MT-026 canvas
corrections: REUSE MT-015 backlinks_panel + MT-024 sidebar - no 3rd duplicate; MT-032's value is loom_address + loom:// chips
content_hash likely backend-computed - READ it, do NOT client-PATCH a hash (verify LoomBlockUpdate); reuse MT-020 canonical_hash + existing sha2
rich_editor/ + graph/canvas_board.rs + loom_graph.rs paths; loom_uri standalone, real-PG NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF
placed_block_id-None skip chip; reuse MT-015 panel author_ids; no Color32 literal; external screenshots

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_844c68bd (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: db43eb96 feat MT-032 everything-is-a-Loom-block addressing + loom:// chips/backlinks
d798f0af fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1/4/5/7/8 PASS; AC-2/3/6-live = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated); 1022 lib + 13 loom_address + 9 integration green; test_theme 19 (palette.accent, no Color32 literal); clippy clean; canvas loom:// chips (loom://ws-32/blk-7, blk-8 green footers) visually verified; screenshot EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.reuse_held: My reuse-not-duplicate steer HELD: NO new backlink_panel.rs (lib.rs documents MT-015 owns it); dispatch_backlink_open bridge routes the EXISTING MT-015 panel's click -> bus CMD_OPEN_DOCUMENT (AC-4). loom_address.rs is the unique deliverable (LoomBlockAddr/ContentHash/loom_uri/resolver) + loom:// chips on canvas (graph/canvas_board.rs) + graph (loom_graph.rs tooltip + backlink_count).
implementation_result.content_hash_verified: CONTRACT CORRECTION (verified, the predicted MT-022 lesson): LoomBlockUpdate has NO writable content_hash (pinned/favorite/title only) - so NO client PATCH; the backend computes content_hash server-side. The live test READS the backend content_hash via getLoomBlock + asserts it equals the local canonical SHA-256 (reused MT-020's canonical algorithm + existing sha2). No fake write.
implementation_result.transient_confirmed: The earlier MT-027 full-suite exit-101 was confirmed a TRANSIENT incremental-cache race ('invalid metadata for crate reqwest') under high concurrency, NOT a code defect (clean recompile + clean re-run).
implementation_result.waiver_granted: backend_client.rs (LiveBlock content_hash read) + interop/interaction_bus.rs (CMD_OPEN_DOCUMENT) + rich_editor/wikilinks/backlinks_panel.rs (dispatch bridge) + graph/canvas_board.rs + loom_graph.rs + tests in allowed_paths.
